title: Aluminum Falls to 3483.23 USD/T on May 7, 2026
summary: Aluminum prices fell to 3483.23 USD/T on May 7, 2026, marking a 1.21% decline from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have risen 0.15%, and are up 44.61% over the past year. UK aluminum futures rebounded to $3,580 per tonne from a three-week low amid expectations of prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East.

title: Comex Aluminium Premiums Beyond August Dip on US Tariff Rollback Bets
summary: Prices of aluminium premium contracts on Comex exchange beyond August have fallen due to bets on US tariff rollbacks. This reflects market expectations of policy changes impacting premiums. Reported by Pratima Desai in London.

title: Steel rebar futures surge above CNY 3,200 per ton, reaching strongest level since August 2025
summary: Steel rebar futures surged above CNY 3,200 per ton, hitting their strongest level since August 2025, supported by robust buying in mainland China as trading resumed after the May Day holiday. The rally was reinforced by a continued drawdown in steel inventories, which have fallen for seven consecutive weeks, pointing to tightening near-term supply conditions.

title: HRC Steel reaches highest level since January 2024 at 1,100 USD/T
summary: Hot-Rolled Coil Steel increased to 1,100.00 USD/T, marking the highest price since January 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, HRC Steel gained 3.79%, demonstrating continued strength in the flat steel market.

title: NMDC raises iron ore prices for fourth consecutive month amid pressure on domestic steel prices
summary: India's state-controlled mining company NMDC raised iron ore prices for the fourth straight month in May, setting prices for 10-40mm Baila 65.5% lump ore at 5,500 rupees/t ($58/t). The price increases come despite domestic steel prices facing pressure from slow demand.

title: North American East Coast export market firms with HMS 85/15 sale at $415.50 per metric ton
summary: The export market on the North American East Coast has been firming after seeing a sale that climbed to $415.50 per metric ton CFR for HMS 85/15. Ternium expects steel demand in Mexico to continue improving through the second quarter as destocking fades.

title: As Electricity Demand Increases, Steel Mills Hold Big Bargaining Chips
source: Association for Iron & Steel Technology - AIST
summary: Fears that electric utilities will prioritize data center power needs over steel mills amid rising electricity demand are likely unfounded, according to two industry executives. Steel mills maintain significant bargaining power due to their established infrastructure and demand profiles.

title: Steel Industry Reps to Deliver Testimony in Section 301 Investigation
source: Association for Iron & Steel Technology - AIST
summary: Representatives from the U.S. steel industry are set to testify on Thursday regarding the harmful impacts of excess industrial capacity and production in 16 economies, including China, the EU, Mexico, and Japan. The testimony is part of a Section 301 investigation.

title: Copper futures remain above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding
summary: Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding in the previous session, supported by signs of improving demand in China despite ongoing headwinds from the Middle East conflict. Copper futures in the US rose to over $6.1 per pound, the highest since reaching a record-close of $6.2 on January 29th as energy prices sank.

title: Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco's smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development is relevant to copper supply dynamics in the market.

title: Copper Futures Price Today
summary: The current price of Copper futures is 6.1890, with a previous close of 6.1243, showing an upward trend in the commodity market.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group and mining firms in Congo related to illicit mineral operations. This action impacts the supply chain for copper and other minerals sourced from the region.

title: Copper Market Supply Constraints: Mitsui & Co and Fortescue Secure Major Offtake Deals
summary: Mitsui & Co acquired 40% offtake rights to Argentina's Josemaria deposit, while Fortescue completed acquisition of Peru's Cañariaco project for approximately $101 million. These deals reflect major companies racing to secure copper supply amid market constraints and record-low treatment charges.

title: Supply Disruptions Impact Copper Production: Chile Refining Challenges and Indonesia's Grasberg Mine
summary: Chile's copper production faces uncertainty due to Middle East conflict disrupting sulphur shipments to China, affecting roughly half of Chile's copper refining operations. Additionally, Indonesia's Grasberg mine continues operating below capacity following a fatal mudslide that triggered a force majeure event.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures traded around $19,150 per tonne, easing from a recent over two-year high near $19,600 due to profit-taking after a sharp rally. Nickel fell to 19,004.38 USD/T on May 7, down 1.02% from the previous day, despite a 10.14% rise over the past month and 22.02% year-over-year. Earlier, prices surged above $19,500 per tonne amid supply disruptions from Indonesian mining quota cuts and a sulfur shortage linked to Strait of Hormuz issues.

title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
summary: Global nickel prices retreated 0.47% to $19,074.88/ton, with Chinese markets at ¥129,735/ton, driven by minor downward movement. On April 28, prices edged up 0.19% to $19,127.31/Ton globally amid bullish momentum from Indonesia's mining quota slashes and a global sulfur shortage due to Middle East tensions. These supply constraints, along with a weaker US dollar, provide a robust price floor for the battery metal.

title: NI1! Charts and Quotes - Futures
summary: The current price of Nickel Futures is 18,967.55 USD/TNE, down 2.32% in the past 24 hours. Broader context includes copper rising as US-China trade tension fears ease and Shanghai metals gaining after China's vows to support industrial growth. An older quote shows 14,996.17 USD/TNE, down 0.93%.

title: Nickel extends gains to near $18,000 on concerns about Indonesian supply disruption
summary: Nickel prices rose 1.8% to $17,900 per ton amid concerns over tighter supply after Indonesia ordered the world’s largest nickel mine to cut production sharply. PT Weda Bay Nickel’s quota is reduced to 12 million tons from 42 million tons. Indonesia plans production quotas of 260-270 million tons of nickel ore this year, below the 2025 target.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures traded around $19,150 per tonne, easing from a two-year high near $19,600 due to profit-taking after a sharp rally. Earlier, prices exceeded $19,500 per tonne driven by supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: Indonesia announced a 2026 nickel ore production target of 250-260 million tons, flat-to-down from 2025, supporting nickel prices toward $20,000 per ton. EV demand shows robust 20% growth, with analysts forecasting 6-7% annual nickel demand growth amid supply discipline.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia plans to cut nickel output in 2026 to support prices by reversing to one-year mining quotas. Actual production lags approved capacity, with current supply still outpacing demand despite quotas.

title: Nickel Enters a New Era as Indonesia Tightens Supply and Prices Surge
summary: Nickel prices have surged to $19,200 per ton, targeting $20,000-$21,000, as Indonesia adopts a dynamic one-year ore quota system like OPEC. Eramet placed Weda Bay on care and maintenance after exhausting its 12-million-ton quota, stabilizing prices for domestic producers.

title: Nickel Industries showcases sustainability in quarterly update
summary: Nickel Industries Limited reported stable operational performance in its Quarterly Activities Report for the period ending 31 December 2025. The update highlights the company's focus on sustainability initiatives amid ongoing operations in Indonesia. This demonstrates commitment to environmental and sustainable practices in nickel production.

title: Nickel Industries grows quarterly volumes, earnings in Indonesia
summary: Nickel Industries has reported growth in quarterly volumes and earnings from its operations in Indonesia. The update likely ties into sustainability efforts as showcased in related reports. This reflects positive market dynamics and operational efficiency in the nickel sector.

title: Let's dive into this new series of articles on sustainability, treatment processes and innovations in nickel production, starting with nickel ores
summary: The Nickel Institute launches a new blog series exploring sustainability, advanced treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production, beginning with nickel ores. This initiative provides expert insights into improving environmental impact and technological advancements. It aims to inform industry stakeholders on sustainable practices.
